basic_window.name: Basic Window Structure
basic_window.description: Standard window with AvaloniaUI namespaces and basic structure
basic_window.xaml:
<Window xmlns="https://github.com/avaloniaui"
        xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml"
        x:Class="MyApp.MainWindow"
        Title="My Application"
        Width="800"
        Height="600">
    <!-- Content goes here -->
</Window>
key_points: Always include AvaloniaUI namespace as default
Include XAML namespace with 'x' prefix
Set appropriate Title, Width, and Height
Use x:Class for code-behind connection

mvvm_window.name: MVVM Window with DataContext
mvvm_window.description: Window configured for MVVM pattern with ViewModel binding
mvvm_window.xaml:
<Window xmlns="https://github.com/avaloniaui"
        xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml"
        xmlns:vm="using:MyApp.ViewModels"
        x:Class="MyApp.Views.MainWindow"
        x:DataType="vm:MainWindowViewModel"
        Title="{Binding Title}"
        Width="800"
        Height="600">
    
    <Design.DataContext>
        <vm:MainWindowViewModel/>
    </Design.DataContext>
    
    <!-- Content with bindings -->
</Window>
key_points: Import ViewModels namespace
Use x:DataType for compiled bindings
Set Design.DataContext for design-time support
Bind window properties to ViewModel

data_binding.name: Data Binding Patterns
data_binding.description: Common data binding scenarios in AvaloniaUI
data_binding.xaml:
<!-- Simple Property Binding -->
<TextBlock Text="{Binding UserName}" />

<!-- Two-Way Binding -->
<TextBox Text="{Binding UserInput, Mode=TwoWay}" />

<!-- Command Binding -->
<Button Content="Save" Command="{Binding SaveCommand}" />

<!-- Converter Binding -->
<TextBlock Text="{Binding Count, Converter={StaticResource CountToStringConverter}}" />

<!-- RelativeSource Binding -->
<TextBlock Text="{Binding DataContext.Title, RelativeSource={RelativeSource AncestorType=Window}}" />
key_points: Use {Binding PropertyName} for simple bindings
Specify Mode=TwoWay for input controls
Commands are bound using Command property
Use converters for data transformation
RelativeSource for ancestor bindings

styles_and_resources.name: Styles and Resources
styles_and_resources.description: Defining and using styles and resources in AvaloniaUI
styles_and_resources.xaml:
<Window.Resources>
    <!-- Colors -->
    <SolidColorBrush x:Key="PrimaryBrush" Color="#FF0078D4" />
    
    <!-- Styles -->
    <Style Selector="Button.primary">
        <Setter Property="Background" Value="{StaticResource PrimaryBrush}" />
        <Setter Property="Foreground" Value="White" />
        <Setter Property="Padding" Value="16,8" />
    </Style>
    
    <!-- Data Templates -->
    <DataTemplate x:Key="PersonTemplate" DataType="{x:Type vm:Person}">
        <StackPanel Orientation="Horizontal">
            <TextBlock Text="{Binding FirstName}" />
            <TextBlock Text="{Binding LastName}" Margin="5,0,0,0" />
        </StackPanel>
    </DataTemplate>
</Window.Resources>

<!-- Usage -->
<Button Classes="primary" Content="Primary Button" />
<ListBox ItemTemplate="{StaticResource PersonTemplate}" />
key_points: Define resources in Resources section
Use Selector syntax for styling
Apply styles using Classes property
DataTemplates for custom item presentation
StaticResource for resource references

layout_grid.name: Grid Layout Pattern
layout_grid.xaml:
<Grid>
    <Grid.RowDefinitions>
        <RowDefinition Height="Auto" />
        <RowDefinition Height="*" />
        <RowDefinition Height="Auto" />
    </Grid.RowDefinitions>
    <Grid.ColumnDefinitions>
        <ColumnDefinition Width="200" />
        <ColumnDefinition Width="*" />
    </Grid.ColumnDefinitions>
    
    <!-- Header spans all columns -->
    <TextBlock Grid.Row="0" Grid.ColumnSpan="2" 
               Text="Application Header" 
               Background="LightGray" 
               Padding="10" />
    
    <!-- Sidebar -->
    <StackPanel Grid.Row="1" Grid.Column="0" 
                Background="LightBlue" 
                Margin="5">
        <Button Content="Menu Item 1" />
        <Button Content="Menu Item 2" />
    </StackPanel>
    
    <!-- Main content -->
    <ContentControl Grid.Row="1" Grid.Column="1" 
                    Content="{Binding CurrentView}" 
                    Margin="5" />
    
    <!-- Footer -->
    <StatusBar Grid.Row="2" Grid.ColumnSpan="2">
        <TextBlock Text="Ready" />
    </StatusBar>
</Grid>
key_points: Define rows and columns first
Use Auto for content-sized rows/columns
Use * for proportional sizing
Grid.ColumnSpan for spanning multiple columns
Grid.Row and Grid.Column for positioning

user_control.name: Custom UserControl
user_control.xaml:
<UserControl xmlns="https://github.com/avaloniaui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.Controls.PersonCard">
    
    <Border BorderBrush="Gray" 
            BorderThickness="1" 
            CornerRadius="5" 
            Padding="10">
        <StackPanel>
            <TextBlock Text="{Binding Name}" 
                       FontWeight="Bold" 
                       FontSize="16" />
            <TextBlock Text="{Binding Email}" 
                       Foreground="Gray" />
            <TextBlock Text="{Binding Phone}" 
                       Foreground="Gray" />
        </StackPanel>
    </Border>
</UserControl>
key_points: UserControl for reusable components
Bind to properties of the DataContext
Use Border for visual boundaries
Style with appropriate colors and spacing

container_queries.name: Container Queries (11.3+)
container_queries.description: CSS-like container queries for responsive styling based on container size
container_queries.xaml:
<Border Name="ResponsiveContainer" Background="LightBlue">
    <Border.Styles>
        <Style Selector="Border">
            <Setter Property="Padding" Value="10" />
        </Style>
        
        <!-- Container query: when container width > 300px -->
        <Style Selector="@container(min-width: 300px) Border">
            <Setter Property="Padding" Value="20" />
            <Setter Property="Background" Value="LightGreen" />
        </Style>
        
        <!-- Container query: when container height > 200px -->
        <Style Selector="@container(min-height: 200px) Border">
            <Setter Property="CornerRadius" Value="10" />
        </Style>
        
        <!-- Named container queries -->
        <Style Selector="@container card (min-width: 250px) Border">
            <Setter Property="BorderThickness" Value="2" />
        </Style>
    </Border.Styles>
    
    <StackPanel>
        <TextBlock Text="Responsive Content" FontWeight="Bold" />
        <TextBlock Text="Container adapts based on size" />
    </StackPanel>
</Border>
key_points: Container queries enable responsive design at component level
Use @container() selector syntax for size-based styling
Support min-width, max-width, min-height, max-height
Named containers allow targeting specific containers
Available in AvaloniaUI 11.3+ as a major new feature

compiled_bindings.name: Compiled Bindings (Performance)
compiled_bindings.xaml:
<UserControl xmlns="https://github.com/avaloniaui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml"
             xmlns:vm="using:MyApp.ViewModels"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.PersonView"
             x:DataType="vm:PersonViewModel">
    
    <!-- Compiled bindings - faster performance -->
    <StackPanel>
        <TextBlock Text="{Binding Name}" FontWeight="Bold" />
        <TextBlock Text="{Binding Email}" />
        <TextBlock Text="{Binding Age, StringFormat='Age: {0}'}" />
        
        <!-- Compiled binding with converter -->
        <Border Background="{Binding IsActive, Converter={StaticResource BoolToBrushConverter}}">
            <TextBlock Text="{Binding Status}" />
        </Border>
        
        <!-- Compiled command binding -->
        <Button Content="Edit" Command="{Binding EditCommand}" />
    </StackPanel>
</UserControl>
key_points: Use x:DataType for compile-time binding validation
Significantly better performance than reflection-based bindings
IntelliSense support for binding properties
Compile-time error checking for invalid bindings
Recommended for all new AvaloniaUI applications

advanced_styling.name: Advanced Styling with CSS-like Selectors
advanced_styling.description: Modern styling using CSS-like selectors and pseudo-classes
advanced_styling.xaml:
<Window.Styles>
    <!-- Basic selector -->
    <Style Selector="Button">
        <Setter Property="Padding" Value="12,6" />
    </Style>
    
    <!-- Class-based selector -->
    <Style Selector="Button.primary">
        <Setter Property="Background" Value="#007ACC" />
        <Setter Property="Foreground" Value="White" />
    </Style>
    
    <!-- Pseudo-class selectors -->
    <Style Selector="Button:pointerover">
        <Setter Property="Background" Value="#005A9E" />
    </Style>
    
    <Style Selector="Button:pressed">
        <Setter Property="Background" Value="#004578" />
    </Style>
    
    <!-- Child combinator -->
    <Style Selector="StackPanel > Button">
        <Setter Property="Margin" Value="0,5" />
    </Style>
    
    <!-- Descendant combinator -->
    <Style Selector="Grid TextBlock">
        <Setter Property="TextAlignment" Value="Center" />
    </Style>
    
    <!-- Attribute selector -->
    <Style Selector="Button[IsDefault=True]">
        <Setter Property="BorderBrush" Value="Orange" />
        <Setter Property="BorderThickness" Value="2" />
    </Style>
    
    <!-- Template selector -->
    <Style Selector="Button /template/ ContentPresenter">
        <Setter Property="Opacity" Value="0.9" />
    </Style>
</Window.Styles>
key_points: CSS-like selector syntax for powerful styling
Pseudo-classes for interactive states (:pointerover, :pressed, :focus)
Child (>) and descendant combinators for targeting
Attribute selectors for conditional styling
Template selectors for styling control templates

spacing_enhancements.name: Enhanced Spacing Properties (11.3+)
spacing_enhancements.description: New spacing properties for layout containers
spacing_enhancements.xaml:
<!-- DockPanel with spacing -->
<DockPanel Spacing="10">
    <Button DockPanel.Dock="Top" Content="Header" />
    <Button DockPanel.Dock="Bottom" Content="Footer" />
    <TextBlock Text="Main content with proper spacing" />
</DockPanel>

<!-- Grid with spacing -->
<Grid RowSpacing="8" ColumnSpacing="12">
    <Grid.RowDefinitions>
        <RowDefinition Height="Auto" />
        <RowDefinition Height="*" />
    </Grid.RowDefinitions>
    <Grid.ColumnDefinitions>
        <ColumnDefinition Width="*" />
        <ColumnDefinition Width="Auto" />
    </Grid.ColumnDefinitions>
    
    <TextBlock Grid.Row="0" Grid.Column="0" Text="Item 1" />
    <Button Grid.Row="0" Grid.Column="1" Content="Action" />
    <TextBlock Grid.Row="1" Grid.ColumnSpan="2" Text="Content" />
</Grid>

<!-- WrapPanel with spacing -->
<WrapPanel Spacing="6">
    <Button Content="Tag 1" />
    <Button Content="Tag 2" />
    <Button Content="Tag 3" />
    <Button Content="Tag 4" />
</WrapPanel>
key_points: DockPanel.Spacing property for automatic spacing
Grid.RowSpacing and Grid.ColumnSpacing properties
WrapPanel.Spacing for uniform item spacing
Eliminates need for manual Margin settings
Available in AvaloniaUI 11.3+ release

popup_overlay.name: Popup with Overlay Layer (11.3+)
popup_overlay.description: Enhanced popup control with overlay layer support
popup_overlay.xaml:
<StackPanel>
    <Button Name="TriggerButton" Content="Show Popup" />
    
    <!-- Popup with overlay layer -->
    <Popup PlacementTarget="{Binding ElementName=TriggerButton}"
           IsOpen="{Binding IsPopupOpen}"
           ShouldUseOverlayLayer="True"
           PlacementMode="Bottom">
        <Border Background="White" 
                BorderBrush="Gray" 
                BorderThickness="1" 
                CornerRadius="4"
                BoxShadow="0 2 8 rgba(0,0,0,0.15)">
            <StackPanel Margin="12">
                <TextBlock Text="Popup Content" FontWeight="Bold" />
                <TextBlock Text="This popup uses the overlay layer" />
                <Button Content="Close" Command="{Binding ClosePopupCommand}" />
            </StackPanel>
        </Border>
    </Popup>
</StackPanel>
key_points: ShouldUseOverlayLayer property for better rendering
Improved popup positioning and clipping
Better support for shadows and effects
Enhanced popup behavior across platforms
New feature in AvaloniaUI 11.3+

value_converter.name: Value Converters (Bindings and Converters)
value_converter.description: Implementing IValueConverter to transform bound data for display; sourced from AvaloniaUI.QuickGuides/BindingsAndConverters
value_converter.xaml:
<!-- Converter definition in XAML resources -->
<Window.Resources>
    <local:BoolToVisibilityConverter x:Key="BoolToVis"/>
    <local:EnumToStringConverter x:Key="EnumToStr"/>
</Window.Resources>

<!-- Using converters in bindings -->
<TextBlock IsVisible="{Binding IsLoggedIn, Converter={StaticResource BoolToVis}}"
           Text="Welcome!"/>
<TextBlock Text="{Binding Status, Converter={StaticResource EnumToStr}}"/>
key_points: Implement IValueConverter for one-way transforms
Implement IMultiValueConverter for multi-binding scenarios
Register converters as StaticResource in Resources section
Use x:DataType for compile-time binding validation
FuncValueConverter<TIn,TOut> is a handy inline alternative

drag_and_drop.name: Drag and Drop
drag_and_drop.description: Implementing drag-and-drop operations between controls; sourced from AvaloniaUI.QuickGuides/DragDropOps
drag_and_drop.xaml:
<!-- Drop target -->
<Border Background="LightBlue"
        AllowDrop="True"
        DragDrop.Drop="OnDrop"
        DragDrop.DragOver="OnDragOver"
        Height="150" Width="200">
    <TextBlock Text="Drop here" HorizontalAlignment="Center" VerticalAlignment="Center"/>
</Border>

<!-- Drag source (initiated in code-behind) -->
<Button Content="Drag me" PointerPressed="OnPointerPressed"/>
key_points: Set AllowDrop="True" on the drop target
Attach DragDrop.Drop and DragDrop.DragOver event handlers
Use DataObject to pass typed data between elements
Call DragDrop.DoDragDrop to initiate the drag operation
Supports DataFormats.Text, Files, and custom formats

file_picker.name: File Picker Dialogs
file_picker.description: Opening and saving files using the platform file picker; sourced from AvaloniaUI.QuickGuides/FileOps
key_points: Use TopLevel.GetTopLevel(this) to access platform services
StorageProvider.OpenFilePickerAsync for opening files
StorageProvider.SaveFilePickerAsync for saving files
FilePickerFileType defines extension filters
Works cross-platform: Windows, macOS, Linux, Browser

clipboard.name: Clipboard Operations
clipboard.description: Reading from and writing to the system clipboard; sourced from AvaloniaUI.QuickGuides/ClipboardOps
key_points: Access clipboard via TopLevel.GetTopLevel(this).Clipboard
SetTextAsync to write, GetTextAsync to read plain text
Use DataObject for rich content (images, files)
ClearAsync clears the clipboard contents
Clipboard access may require user gesture on some platforms

localization.name: Localization / i18n
localization.description: Implementing multi-language support using ResX resource files; sourced from AvaloniaUI.QuickGuides/Localization
key_points: Add .resx files per culture: Resources.resx, Resources.de.resx, Resources.fr.resx
Reference strings via {x:Static loc:Resources.Key} in XAML
Set Thread.CurrentUICulture to switch language at runtime
Consider NuGet packages like ResX.Generator for source generation
Avalonia supports RTL layout via FlowDirection property

splash_screen.name: Splash Screen
splash_screen.description: Displaying a splash screen on application startup; sourced from AvaloniaUI.QuickGuides/SplashScreen
splash_screen.xaml:
<!-- SplashWindow.axaml -->
<Window xmlns="https://github.com/avaloniaui"
        xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml"
        x:Class="MyApp.SplashWindow"
        Width="400" Height="250"
        WindowStartupLocation="CenterScreen"
        SystemDecorations="None"
        TransparencyLevelHint="Transparent"
        Background="Transparent">
    <Border Background="#FF1976D2" CornerRadius="12">
        <StackPanel VerticalAlignment="Center" HorizontalAlignment="Center" Spacing="16">
            <Image Source="/Assets/logo.png" Width="96" Height="96"/>
            <TextBlock Text="Loading…" Foreground="White" FontSize="18"/>
            <ProgressBar IsIndeterminate="True" Width="200"/>
        </StackPanel>
    </Border>
</Window>
key_points: Show SplashWindow before the main window in OnFrameworkInitializationCompleted
Use SystemDecorations=None and transparent background for a modern look
Perform heavy initialization on a background thread (Task.Run)
Close the splash and show MainWindow once init is complete
Optionally use IClassicDesktopStyleApplicationLifetime.MainWindow

native_menu.name: Native OS Menu
native_menu.description: Adding a native operating-system menu (macOS menu bar, tray icon, etc.); sourced from AvaloniaUI.QuickGuides/NativeMenuOps
native_menu.xaml:
<NativeMenu.Menu>
    <NativeMenu>
        <NativeMenuItem Header="File">
            <NativeMenuItem.Menu>
                <NativeMenu>
                    <NativeMenuItem Header="New" Gesture="Ctrl+N" Command="{Binding NewCommand}"/>
                    <NativeMenuItem Header="Open…" Gesture="Ctrl+O" Command="{Binding OpenCommand}"/>
                    <NativeMenuItemSeparator/>
                    <NativeMenuItem Header="Quit" Gesture="Ctrl+Q" Command="{Binding QuitCommand}"/>
                </NativeMenu>
            </NativeMenuItem.Menu>
        </NativeMenuItem>
    </NativeMenu>
</NativeMenu.Menu>
key_points: NativeMenu renders as the OS native menu bar on macOS
Use NativeMenuItem.Gesture for keyboard shortcuts
NativeMenuItemSeparator adds a visual divider
Attach to Application for global menu (macOS) or Window for window menu
Combine with TrayIcon for system tray support on Windows/Linux

custom_control.name: Custom TemplatedControl
custom_control.description: Creating a fully custom control with a default control template; sourced from AvaloniaUI.QuickGuides/CustomControl
custom_control.xaml:
<!-- Styles/MyRatingControl.axaml (default template) -->
<Styles xmlns="https://github.com/avaloniaui"
        xmlns:local="using:MyApp.Controls">
    <Style Selector="local|MyRatingControl">
        <Setter Property="Template">
            <ControlTemplate>
                <StackPanel Orientation="Horizontal" TemplateBinding="Value">
                    <!-- Star icons here -->
                </StackPanel>
            </ControlTemplate>
        </Setter>
    </Style>
</Styles>
key_points: Inherit from TemplatedControl for full templating support
Define StyledProperty for each bindable property
Provide a default ControlTemplate in a Styles file
Override OnApplyTemplate to access named template parts
Add the Styles include to App.axaml for global availability

items_repeater_list.name: Virtualized List with ItemsRepeater
items_repeater_list.description: Building high-performance scrollable lists using ItemsRepeater with virtualization; sourced from AvaloniaUI.QuickGuides/MakingLists
items_repeater_list.xaml:
<ScrollViewer>
    <ItemsRepeater ItemsSource="{Binding LargeCollection}">
        <ItemsRepeater.Layout>
            <StackLayout Spacing="4" Orientation="Vertical"/>
        </ItemsRepeater.Layout>
        <ItemsRepeater.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate x:DataType="local:MyItem">
                <Border Background="White" CornerRadius="4" Padding="12" Margin="4,2">
                    <StackPanel Orientation="Horizontal" Spacing="8">
                        <Image Source="{Binding Thumbnail}" Width="32" Height="32"/>
                        <StackPanel>
                            <TextBlock Text="{Binding Title}" FontWeight="Bold"/>
                            <TextBlock Text="{Binding Subtitle}" Opacity="0.6"/>
                        </StackPanel>
                    </StackPanel>
                </Border>
            </DataTemplate>
        </ItemsRepeater.ItemTemplate>
    </ItemsRepeater>
</ScrollViewer>
key_points: Wrap ItemsRepeater in ScrollViewer for scrollable lists
StackLayout, WrapLayout, and UniformGridLayout are built-in layouts
Use x:DataType on DataTemplate for compiled bindings
ItemsRepeater virtualizes by default; ideal for thousands of items
No selection built-in – implement selection in your ViewModel
